metadata.title: Who was Max Planck?
metadata.color_semantics: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent — terracotta on quantum moments and Planck curve; SLATE #8A8678 for classical/RJ curve
metadata.greeting_note: German hello — Planck was born in Kiel, Germany. Wagwan check: charsum('who-was-max-planck') % 10 = 9, not 0.
metadata.note: COLD OPEN LAW: B00 = ClaudeComposerAsk. IN-FOR-BEAR LAW: B00 narration opens 'This is Liam, in for Bear.' ILLUSTRATE LAW: UI only at B00, B03, B08, B09. ASK→RESULT LAW: B03=ask micro-beat, B04=Manim result. HANDOFF LAW: B08='Your turn.' OUTRO LAW: B09=ClaudeTitleOutro restates title. Spine: B00 cold open · B01 the problem · B02 the gambit (Manim E=hν) · B03 ask · B04 result (Manim Planck curve) · B05 reluctant revolutionary · B06 legacy · B07 the man · B08 handoff · B09 outro.
metadata.description_blurb: Max Planck didn't mean to start a revolution. He was a classicist trying to solve one stubborn problem: how does a hot object glow? What he found broke classical physics — and built everything that followed.

source_of_truth: beat_sheet.json — edit the sheet, not this file
